# Roadmap

## Milestone 0: Compile-Stable Foundation

- SwiftPM package, docs, CI skeleton.
- Core models, mock agent, shell/file/git tools.
- Auto safety policy and reviewer protocol.
- Persistence and secret-store API.
- Unit tests for direct command execution and safety behavior.
- Current status: shell, file read/write, apply patch, git status/diff, file/hunk-level git stage/restore, local git commit, named-remote git push, GitHub PR creation, PR view/comments, PR checks, PR diff, PR checkout, PR comments, PR reviews, PR reviewer requests/removals, PR label add/removal, PR merge/automerge, and git worktree list/create/open/remove/prune are implemented with unit coverage. CI runs on pushes, pull requests, and merge-group candidates, and a serialized merge-train workflow is available for agent PRs labeled `merge-train`.

## Milestone 1: Native Workspace UI

- QuillUI shell with sidebar, top bar, composer, transcript, and tool cards.
- Project picker and model picker.
- Settings with OAuth/dev override and mode selection.
- Native smoke tests.
- Current status: testable workspace state model, persisted config/thread/project bootstrap, bounded broad-to-specific project instruction loading from root and nested `.quillcode/rules`, bounded memory loading from `~/.quillcode/memories` and project `.quillcode/memories`, explicit `/remember text` global memory writes, agent-callable `host.memory.remember` writes for user-requested durable preferences/facts, and Memories-pane Forget action with basic credential rejection, bounded project-local extension manifest discovery for plugins, skills, and MCP servers, explicit MCP stdio server start/stop lifecycle controls with bounded `initialize`/`tools/list` readiness probes, generic allowlisted MCP `tools/call` routing for Ready servers, HTML surface contract, Playwright harness, smoke script, and SwiftUI desktop shell exist for project rail, visible New chat/Search/Open project/Browser/Terminal/Memories/Extensions/Activity sidebar actions, desktop folder picking, typed Local/SSH Remote project metadata, `Project: Add SSH Remote...` and `/ssh user@host:/path` registration, project row actions for New chat/Refresh context/Rename/Remove from list with SSH Remote context refresh over SSH, SSH Remote git status/diff/stage/restore/commit/push/PR/worktree execution, bounded SSH Remote file read/write, SSH Remote apply-patch and review actions with remote diff refresh, thread rename/duplicate/pin/archive/unarchive/delete controls, bulk chat select/select-all/pin/unpin/archive/unarchive/delete controls, pinned/recent/archived chat grouping, thread search that excludes hidden tool-feedback state and supports keyboard result highlighting plus Enter selection, active-chat `Cmd+F` transcript Find, grouped/ranked command palette with keyboard navigation, registry-backed primary keyboard shortcuts, command-derived `Cmd+/` Keyboard Shortcuts discoverability, local environment action commands from `.quillcode/actions` and `.quillcode/local-env` with optional display/environment/working-directory/timeout metadata sidecars plus workspace-bounded shell cwd, structured environment overrides, redacted environment values in transcript/tool-card payloads, and timeout routing, browser preview state/comments/snapshots with bounded HTTP(S) HTML fetch upgrades, explicit inspection-depth labels, and structured browser open/inspect tools, multiline composer parity in SwiftUI/static HTML/Playwright with shared-catalog keyboard-navigable slash suggestions, Shift+Enter newlines, Enter-to-send, active-run Stop, structured `/pr create|view|checks|diff|checkout|comment|review|reviewers|labels|merge` commands routed through the same local/SSH Remote GitHub PR tools as agent calls, and structured `/worktree create|open|remove|prune` commands routed through the same local/SSH Remote worktree flow as visible UI, clustered in-app top bar with bounded context/status labels, instruction/memory/Computer Use status, and native macOS menu-bar widget, derived right-side Activity pane for deterministic or model-authored task-plan/current-task/source/tool/artifact/latest-answer/handoff state with command-driven collapsible sections, chronological event-driven transcript, transcript copy actions with visible copied feedback, user-message Use as draft, assistant response feedback actions backed by thread events, latest-assistant Retry action over the shared retry command, estimated context pressure banner with Compact context/New thread/Fork from last actions, deterministic compact-context continuation threads, incremental agent-run progress, bounded multi-step agent tool continuation, tool-card presentation with human final-answer bubbles, stopped-tool cleanup, artifact preview chips plus bounded image previews with shared type/source metadata, and collapsed raw details for successful tools, integrated workspace terminal command history with Up/Down recall and live stdout/stderr streaming, persistent per-project cwd and environment deltas, running/done/failed/stopped lifecycle, and Stop controls, git diff review summaries with post-patch refresh, file/hunk Stage/Restore controls, and file-scoped review notes, git worktree command-palette actions, safe dry-run prune action, review/confirm stale-worktree prune dialog, create/open/remove dialogs, open/remove dialogs with known-worktree pickers, local and SSH Remote picker loading/error/retry states without transcript tool-card noise, and create/open-time local/SSH Remote worktree project/thread handoff, slash commands for core workspace actions, project management, memory visibility/writing/forgetting, context compaction, thread lifecycle, and local environment scripts, searchable grouped model selection with keyboard result highlighting and Enter selection, persistent favorites, recent-model surfacing, current/default/recommended/favorite badges, deterministic model metadata rows, inline model detail browsing, mode switching, and native developer settings. The desktop app seeds the current working directory as the initial project and runs tools from the selected local project path.
- SSH Remote chat execution now uses the same noninteractive SSH path as the integrated terminal for `host.shell.run`, bounded `host.file.read`/`host.file.write`, `host.apply_patch`, `host.git.status`/`host.git.diff`, file/hunk `host.git.stage`/`host.git.restore`, `host.git.commit`, `host.git.push`, `host.git.pr.create`, `host.git.pr.view`, `host.git.pr.checks`, `host.git.pr.diff`, `host.git.pr.checkout`, `host.git.pr.comment`, `host.git.pr.review`, `host.git.pr.reviewers`, `host.git.pr.labels`, `host.git.pr.merge`, and `host.git.worktree.*`, while environment tools are filtered out for remote projects and guarded at execution time if a model still asks for one. Explicit SSH Remote Refresh context reads bounded remote `AGENTS.md`, `.quillcode/rules.md`, `.quillcode/instructions.md`, and project `.quillcode/memories` files into the selected thread context. Remote shell/file/patch/git tool cards and terminal entries also carry explicit execution-context chips/rails so transcript history remains clear after project switches. SSH Remote terminal commands now preserve cwd and exported/unset environment deltas across commands through stripped marker metadata.

## Milestone 2: TrustedRouter Runtime

- OAuth PKCE and delegated key storage.
- Streaming chat client.
- Live model catalog native UI.
- Auto reviewer using `glm-5.2` with `kimi-k2.6` fallback in the native app.
- Current status: live TrustedRouter adapter exists behind CLI `--live`; desktop env/secret-key runtime selection, OAuth-first auth mode state, native loopback PKCE sign-in, pure authorize/exchange/userinfo OAuth client, automatic delegated key storage, non-secret signed-in account display, secondary developer key/base-URL settings, live model-catalog refresh, CLI key-management commands, incremental agent progress callbacks, bounded multi-step tool continuation, streamed action-text transport, canonical action parsing with near-miss schema alias normalization, visible Streaming status, safe streamed assistant drafts for `say` actions, shared actionable runtime issue UI for sign-in/key/rate-limit/network/empty-response/malformed-action failures, Retry-last-turn recovery from transient runtime failures, malformed-response and rate-limit recovery through the searchable model picker, and redacted runtime diagnostics with parsed rate-limit metadata in Settings are implemented. Account recovery telemetry and provider status diagnostics beyond local runtime state remain the next runtime polish.

## Milestone 3: Codex Workflow Parity

- Full PTY terminal sessions with job control and TUI handling, richer worktree branch lifecycle polish, local env orchestration, and richer GitHub review workflows.
- Per-file project-instruction scopes now attach to loaded `AGENTS.md`, `.quillcode/rules.md`, and `.quillcode/instructions.md` records, flow into TrustedRouter hidden system context, and show in Activity sources. Activity also surfaces duplicate-scope and nested-override diagnostics so users can audit likely rule precedence issues; semantic contradiction detection and richer conflict review UI remain pending.
- Browser rendering adapter, live DOM/page inspection implementation for dynamic pages, richer browser comments, and browser-open snapshot fetching from agent-driven navigation. The live DOM capture contract now exists behind `BrowserLiveDOMCapturing`, and the macOS desktop app can upgrade fetched HTTP(S) pages through an offscreen WebKit rendered-DOM capture that reuses WebKit's persistent cookie/session data store by default. A reusable visible macOS WebKit browser-session window can now be opened from the browser pane, menu bar, or command palette to sign into sites using the same profile. Multi-tab visible session management, richer session synchronization, and Linux/browser-process adapters still need implementation.
- Computer Use platform backends, app approvals, and agent wiring. macOS now has permission-aware status, screenshot/input primitives, Settings onboarding actions, and structured agent tools for screenshot/click/type/scroll/move/key dispatch through the active backend; Linux backend, richer onboarding, and multi-step model feedback over screenshot results remain.
- Full embedded PDF/doc/sheet/slide/appshot artifact renderers, marketplace plugin/skill lifecycle, MCP streaming beyond the generic verified-server call path, and richer prompt/resource UX. Structured PDF/document/spreadsheet/presentation/appshot artifact cards now exist in the shared tool-card surface, including `.appshot.json` bundle classification without raw JSON text-preview noise. Project extension manifests can expose explicit bounded install/update commands that run through the same shell tool-card path and refresh project metadata afterward. MCP resource reads, prompt gets, and bounded per-tool input schema summaries now run through allowlisted Ready-server agent tools and the Extensions UI, building on the tested discovery UI contract. Ready MCP resources and prompts are directly actionable from the Extensions pane and command palette, with results recorded as normal tool cards and assistant summaries. Explicit memory editing and Forget actions now cover global, local project, and SSH Remote project memory files. Richer memory redaction/Chronicle jobs, fully autonomous memory inference, conflict review, project-memory review UX, and automations remain pending.
